subject: Physics
title: Sublimation: Solid to Gas
description: A micro-lesson that makes Sublimation: Solid to Gas usable.

hook.text: Dry ice doesn't melt—it skips liquid entirely and turns straight into gas. This is sublimation.

buildup.text: At normal air pressure, solid carbon dioxide can't exist as a liquid. It jumps from solid to gas.

discovery.text: Sublimation happens when conditions skip the liquid phase entirely—low pressure or specific temperature ranges.

twist.text: Freeze-drying food uses sublimation. Frozen water in the food sublimates away, preserving texture and flavor.

climax.text: Snow can sublimate on cold, dry, sunny days—disappearing without melting first.

punchline.text: Solid to gas. No liquid in between. Sublimation skips a step.

quiz.question: What is sublimation?
options: A solid turning directly into gas without becoming liquid
A gas turning directly into solid
